findings.message: No explicit output format section; output structure is described only in vague prose ('structured, actionable, and focused') without a concrete template.
Vague quantifier 'proper' in 'proper error handling, type safety, and defensive programming' — does not specify what proper means in this context.
Vague quantifier 'proper' in 'proper separation of concerns and loose coupling'.
Vague quantifier 'appropriate' in 'code includes appropriate comments and documentation'.
Missing required `name` field in command frontmatter; command registration may fall back to filename or fail entirely.
Deprecated command stub has no allowed-tools declaration.
Deprecated command stub has no empty input handling. The command always produces the same redirect message regardless of input.

The `matcher` field on a SessionStart hook entry is non-standard; Claude Code matchers apply to PostToolUse tool names only. This field is likely silently ignored, making the hook always fire. hooks-cursor.json correctly omits the matcher.
Vague quantifier 'appropriately-scoped' — leaves scope judgement undefined for the agent.

The `matcher` field on a SessionStart hook entry is non-standard; Claude Code matchers apply to PostToolUse tool names only. This field is likely silently ignored, making the hook always fire. hooks-cursor.json correctly omits the matcher.
